changes.before: Something obvious to brace against. The room gave her shelves instead...
changes.after: Some theater to brace against. The room offered shelves instead...
changes.effect: Trimmed opener and tightened the camera.

changes.before: Jandice had gone proud and careful again, all her delighted spillage packed back behind a noblewoman's posture...
changes.after: Jandice kept one hand on the door. Proud. Careful.
changes.effect: Cut explanation and kept the visible posture.

changes.before: Jaina watched the bow more than the words... student dismissed from a seminar she wanted to stay in.
changes.after: Warmth touched Jandice's face and vanished. She bowed and left.

changes.before: Prepared tissue. Repeated exposure. Pattern retention.
changes.after: Prepared host. Repeated teaching. The body keeping the lesson.
changes.effect: Pulled the mechanism back into occult-mage language.

changes.before: She drew another branch, narrower. Jaina wrote nothing. The board was already too full.
changes.after: She drew another branch. Her hand stayed raised over the slate.

changes.before: The shift from board to table felt deliberate, the way a lecture changes shape when the proof is done and the argument begins.
changes.after: The shift from board to table changed the room with him. Proof done. Argument beginning.
changes.effect: Regrounded the transition inside the moment.

changes.before: The distinction opened under her feet. Low village dead. The rough answerables. And above them, a second aim.
changes.after: The distinction opened under her feet. Low village dead. The rough answerables. And above them, a second aim.
changes.effect: Collapsed the beat into one compact paragraph block.

changes.before: They were correct to fear that line...
changes.after: They were right to fear that line...
changes.effect: Made the lecture hit cleaner without changing meaning.
Cut the extra sting after the accusation had already landed.

changes.before: the world outside our study chambers moved too slowly for the work at hand
changes.after: the world outside our studies too slow for the work at hand
changes.effect: Trimmed document phrasing while keeping Kel'Thuzad's lecture cadence.

changes.before: Same schooling. Same gesture corrected two different ways.
The question came too fast. She turned.
changes.effect: Let the question snap straight into her answer.

changes.before: The line between patronage and ownership would interest only record-keepers.
changes.effect: Cut a neat but unnecessary flourish.

changes.before: He said it with the same calm he used for everything. The worst part was that it might even be sound advice.
changes.after: Then stop the machine you can touch. Leave the cosmology for later.
changes.effect: Trusted the line instead of glossing it.

changes.before: Formal beauty, never moral... the distance between a shuffling village dead and a preserved intelligence narrowing from impossibility to method.
changes.after: For one heartbeat she followed it all the way through. The body taught to hold its ending...
changes.effect: Kept the horror and cut the essay tail.

changes.before: No one in Dalaran will know what to do with the truth.
changes.effect: Confirmed Jaina's spoken phrasing stays humane and plain despite high-stakes exposition.

flags: Lines 145-179: kept 'consent' and 'continuity of self' because the scene is a legal-ethical argument between senior mages, not accidental report voice.
Lines 259-267: kept 'policy' and 'worth studying' in Kel'Thuzad's mouth because his voice sheet explicitly allows calm lecture-room monstrosity.
Lines 329-341: kept 'logistics' and 'preparation' because Jaina is translating revelation into operational terms under pressure, not drifting into memo-speak.

observations: Opening dropped from a four-beat expectation ladder to a tighter three-beat setup before the room description.
Jandice's exit lost the seminar metaphor and now leaves on the visible bow alone.
Kel'Thuzad's first visual lost redundant 'Alive. Human.' throat-clearing and lands on the robe and body instead.
Board-side mechanics lost extra branch and 'board already too full' narration; the scene keeps moving.
The village/selfhood distinction now lands in one compact paragraph instead of a stair-step restatement.
The Dalaran debate no longer detours into the removed book-handoff beat.
The accusation mirror lost the trailing 'Shall I call that clean?' jab because the blow already lands.
The Antonidas/Kel'Thuzad shelf mirror lost duplicate explanatory sentences after the sigil detail made the point.
The Northrend patronage section dropped the record-keepers aside and stayed on scale, authorship, and actionable threat.
The recurrence reveal lost one full layer of theoretical gloss while keeping the same horror beat.
Ending anticipation cut excess throat-clearing before the ward pulses and Sylvanas reveal.
Clinical trio 'prepared tissue / repeated exposure / pattern retention' was rewritten into mage-voice phrasing.
Document-voice lecture gloss at the board-to-desk turn was replaced with a concrete room-change beat.
Multiple attribution footnotes were removed so dialogue carries its own force.
Overwritten reaction tags after Kel'Thuzad's strongest lines were cut instead of restated.
The opening lost one vague 'Something...' construction and one extra explanatory bridge.
Filter-word count dropped from 6 to 2 across the chapter.
Vague 'something' usage dropped from 2 to 1.
'like' similes dropped from 2 to 1.
Contact-verb repetition removed the only lingering 'pressed' instance in narration metrics.
Academic-register density dropped from 24 tracked hits to 20 without flattening the mage scene.
The chapter now ends on the concrete ward-approach pattern instead of an extra explanatory run-up.
Quoted speech was scanned end-to-end after the rewrite; no spoken lines use banned jargon such as actionable, vector, optimal, ingestion, or propagation.
Jaina's speech stays exact but human; her sharpest lines are short ('Not poison.' / 'Do not compare us.' / 'They were people.').
Kel'Thuzad keeps deliberate lecture-room coldness, which matches his voice sheet rather than sounding bureaucratic by accident.
Potentially formal ethical vocabulary was kept only where the scene is explicitly debating personhood, consent, and civic violation.
The line about the world outside their studies was tightened so it sounds like a finished thought, not a policy memo.
No spoken line needed document-register substitution after the final pass; the remaining formality is character-specific, not slop.
